task_completion.visual_evidence: The eQUEST application is open, and the window title or project tree clearly displays '4StoreyBuilding'.
A PV Generator component is visible in the eQUEST detailed interface tree (e.g., under Utility & Economics) or a new PV Generator properties dialog is open.

task_completion.description: Configure the PV generator with the specified capacity, tilt, azimuth, and efficiency
task_completion.visual_evidence: The properties dialog for the PV Generator shows Capacity set to 25.0 (kW), Tilt set to 20 (degrees), Azimuth set to 180 (degrees), and Efficiency set to 0.18.
The eQUEST simulation dialog is visible, showing the progress or successful completion of the annual simulation.

task_completion.description: Ensure simulation results reflect the generated electricity
task_completion.visual_evidence: An eQUEST results report (such as the Electric Meter report or PV Performance report) is open, showing non-zero generated electricity from the PV system.
The agent is seen clicking the 'Save' icon, using the File -> Save menu, or a save confirmation dialog is visible.

integrity.description: The agent used the eQUEST graphical user interface to configure the PV system
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with eQUEST dialog boxes and menus, rather than editing the .inp file directly in a text editor.

integrity.description: Simulation results are viewed within the eQUEST application
integrity.visual_evidence: The results showing generated electricity are displayed in eQUEST's built-in report viewer (SIM file viewer), not a separate text editor or mocked file.

integrity.description: The agent actually entered the parameters into the correct fields
integrity.visual_evidence: The parameters (25.0, 20, 180, 0.18) are visible in their respective input fields in the PV Generator properties dialog, not just written in a scratchpad.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target PV system parameters are realistic and verified: 25.0 kW capacity, 20-degree tilt, 180-degree azimuth (True South), and 0.18 efficiency. The building model is a real calibrated DOE-2 model (4StoreyBuilding.inp, 94,825 bytes). Look for these exact parameter values in the eQUEST PV Generator properties.
